__comment: Server monitoring dashboard shipped with Gefieder, provisioned from grafana/provisioning/dashboards. It charts the host counters the server-stats collector writes into the ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA} schema (see postgresql/initdb/gf_0007_create_server_stats.sh) to help size a future server. Layout: a top row of stat numbers (available CPU cores and memory, plus the network traffic sent/received totalled over the selected window), then time-series trends for CPU, memory, disk throughput, network throughput and storage. The ${APP_NAME}-postgresql datasource uid and the schema name are substituted at build time by grafana/render.sh, so this file is a template: do not load it into Grafana directly.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT host_nproc AS "Cores"
FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
WHERE host_nproc IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY sampled_at DESC
LIMIT 1

SELECT host_mem_total_bytes AS "Memory"
FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
WHERE host_mem_total_bytes IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY sampled_at DESC
LIMIT 1

-- Total outgoing traffic over the selected window: sum of the positive per-sample
-- deltas of the monotonic net_tx counter. Reach one sample before the window so the
-- first in-window delta is not lost, then clip the sum to the window.
SELECT sum(d_tx) AS "Sent"
FROM (
  SELECT
    sampled_at,
    GREATEST(net_tx_bytes - lag(net_tx_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0) AS d_tx
  FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
  WHERE sampled_at >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz - interval '1 hour' AND sampled_at <= $__timeTo()
) d
WHERE $__timeFilter(sampled_at)

-- Total incoming traffic over the selected window (mirror of the sent total above).
SELECT sum(d_rx) AS "Received"
FROM (
  SELECT
    sampled_at,
    GREATEST(net_rx_bytes - lag(net_rx_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0) AS d_rx
  FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
  WHERE sampled_at >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz - interval '1 hour' AND sampled_at <= $__timeTo()
) d
WHERE $__timeFilter(sampled_at)

SELECT time, "CPU cores busy" FROM (
  SELECT
    sampled_at AS "time",
    GREATEST(cpu_usage_usec - lag(cpu_usage_usec) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0)
      / NULLIF(extract(epoch FROM sampled_at - lag(sampled_at) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at)) * 1e6, 0) AS "CPU cores busy"
  FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
  -- Reach one sample before the visible window so lag() gives the first shown point a
  -- real predecessor instead of a NULL rate; the outer filter then clips to the window.
  WHERE sampled_at >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz - interval '1 hour' AND sampled_at <= $__timeTo()
) s
WHERE $__timeFilter(time)
ORDER BY time

SELECT
  sampled_at AS "time",
  mem_current_bytes AS "Memory used"
FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
WHERE $__timeFilter(sampled_at)
ORDER BY sampled_at

SELECT time, "Read", "Write" FROM (
  SELECT
    sampled_at AS "time",
    GREATEST(io_read_bytes - lag(io_read_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0)
      / NULLIF(extract(epoch FROM sampled_at - lag(sampled_at) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at)), 0) AS "Read",
    GREATEST(io_write_bytes - lag(io_write_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0)
      / NULLIF(extract(epoch FROM sampled_at - lag(sampled_at) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at)), 0) AS "Write"
  FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
  -- Reach one sample before the visible window so lag() gives the first shown point a
  -- real predecessor instead of a NULL rate; the outer filter then clips to the window.
  WHERE sampled_at >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz - interval '1 hour' AND sampled_at <= $__timeTo()
) s
WHERE $__timeFilter(time)
ORDER BY time

SELECT time, "Sent", "Received" FROM (
  SELECT
    sampled_at AS "time",
    GREATEST(net_tx_bytes - lag(net_tx_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0)
      / NULLIF(extract(epoch FROM sampled_at - lag(sampled_at) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at)), 0) AS "Sent",
    GREATEST(net_rx_bytes - lag(net_rx_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at), 0)
      / NULLIF(extract(epoch FROM sampled_at - lag(sampled_at) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at)), 0) AS "Received"
  FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
  -- Reach one sample before the visible window so lag() gives the first shown point a
  -- real predecessor instead of a NULL rate; the outer filter then clips to the window.
  WHERE sampled_at >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz - interval '1 hour' AND sampled_at <= $__timeTo()
) s
WHERE $__timeFilter(time)
ORDER BY time

SELECT time, "Database", "All volumes", "Temp/spill" FROM (
  SELECT
    time,
    -- Carry the last non-NULL value forward: the size columns are only probed every few
    -- minutes and are NULL in between, so a short window can hold only NULLs. Grouping by
    -- the running count of non-NULL samples puts each NULL run in the group of the value
    -- before it; taking that group's (single) non-NULL value forward-fills the gaps. Done
    -- per column because the probes for each may land on different rows.
    max(db_size_bytes)     OVER (PARTITION BY db_grp)   AS "Database",
    max(volume_size_bytes) OVER (PARTITION BY vol_grp)  AS "All volumes",
    max(temp_size_bytes)   OVER (PARTITION BY temp_grp) AS "Temp/spill"
  FROM (
    SELECT
      sampled_at AS time, db_size_bytes, volume_size_bytes, temp_size_bytes,
      count(db_size_bytes)     OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at) AS db_grp,
      count(volume_size_bytes) OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at) AS vol_grp,
      count(temp_size_bytes)   OVER (ORDER BY sampled_at) AS temp_grp
    FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.host_sample
    -- Reach back far enough to include the previous size probe before the window starts.
    WHERE sampled_at >= $__timeFrom()::timestamptz - interval '1 hour' AND sampled_at <= $__timeTo()
  ) filled
) s
WHERE $__timeFilter(time)
ORDER BY time

SELECT
  schemaname || '.' || relname AS "Table",
  (max(seq_scan) - min(seq_scan)) AS "Seq scans",
  (max(idx_scan) - min(idx_scan)) AS "Index scans",
  ((max(seq_scan) - min(seq_scan)) + (max(idx_scan) - min(idx_scan))) AS "Total scans",
  round(((max(seq_scan) - min(seq_scan)) + (max(idx_scan) - min(idx_scan))) / GREATEST(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(sampled_at) - min(sampled_at)) / 86400.0, 1), 3) AS "Scans/day",
  max(n_live_tup) AS "Live rows",
  (max(heap_blks_read) - min(heap_blks_read)) AS "Heap blocks read",
  (max(idx_blks_read) - min(idx_blks_read)) AS "Index blocks read"
FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.table_sample
WHERE $__timeFilter(sampled_at)
GROUP BY schemaname, relname
ORDER BY "Total scans" DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 20

SELECT
  left(query, 2000) AS "Query",
  (max(calls) - min(calls)) AS "Runs",
  round(((max(total_exec_time) - min(total_exec_time)) / 1000)::numeric, 3) AS "Time sec",
  round(((max(calls) - min(calls)) / GREATEST(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(sampled_at) - min(sampled_at)) / 86400.0, 1))::numeric, 3) AS "Runs/day",
  round(((max(total_exec_time) - min(total_exec_time)) / 1000 / GREATEST(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(sampled_at) - min(sampled_at)) / 86400.0, 1))::numeric, 3) AS "Time sec/day",
  CASE WHEN max(calls) - min(calls) > 0 THEN
    round(((max(total_exec_time) - min(total_exec_time)) / 1000 / (max(calls) - min(calls)))::numeric, 3)
    ELSE NULL END AS "Avg sec"
FROM ${SERVER_STATS_SCHEMA}.query_sample
WHERE $__timeFilter(sampled_at)
GROUP BY queryid, query
ORDER BY "Runs" DESC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 20

title: Server monitoring
